methodsAttempted.query: site:gatech.edu Georgia Tech generative AI policy students academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official OIT AI Standards and Guidance page before Firecrawl credits were exhausted.

methodsAttempted.query: site:gatech.edu Georgia Tech AI Standards and Guidance Office of Information Technology
methodsAttempted.notes: Official-domain search found OIT AI guidance, GenAI guidance, academic guidance, the Institutional AI Register page, CTL course-policy guidance, and draft Provost PDFs.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ctl.gatech.edu Georgia Tech GenAI syllabus course policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Official CTL search identified the public Establishing Course Policies page and an official SharePoint AI policy document link that returned HTTP 403.

methodsAttempted.query: site:provost.gatech.edu Georgia Tech AI Policy draft 10.14.2025
methodsAttempted.notes: Official Provost PDFs were found, but the policy PDF was marked DRAFT with effective date TBD and was excluded from approved findings.

artifacts.summary: Official-source-only discovery found public Georgia Tech OIT and CTL pages about AI guidance, security review, tool availability, academic use, and course policy expectations. A Provost AI policy PDF was rejected because it is marked draft with an effective date still to be determined, and a CTL-linked SharePoint AI policy document was rejected as inaccessible after returning HTTP 403.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Institutional AI Register | Office of Information Technology
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All technology services and tools offered at the Institute must undergo a comprehensive review, which includes a series of third-party risk assessments, privacy and security analysis, contractual negotiations, financial and technical support impact assessments, and data stewardship approvals. artifacts.sourceTitle: Establishing Course Policies - Center for Teaching and Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Let your students know your expectations for how they should collaborate with and cite their use of Generative AI (GenAI) platforms, such as Microsoft Copilot or any other approved systems. artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow privacy claim directly supported by the OIT AI Standards and Guidance page.
Approved as a tool-specific treatment claim because the OIT page states DeepSeek is prohibited for Georgia Tech use cases involving work, research, or GT data.

Approved as a narrow academic-integrity communication claim supported by OIT's GenAI guidance.

artifacts.title: Georgia Institute of Technology AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Georgia Tech's public official AI guidance currently provides source-backed claims on privacy limits for AI tools, DeepSeek prohibition, AI tool security review, classroom communication about acceptable GenAI use, CTL syllabus guidance, and consultation for AI assessment integrations. Draft Provost policy material and an inaccessible SharePoint document were excluded from approved findings.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Draft policy PDFs were not treated as binding policy.
The CTL-linked SharePoint policy document returned HTTP 403 and was not accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
